changelog: All redundant exercises removed (Overhead Cable Ext D3 M1, Prone Y-T-W D3+D6 M1, Cable Woodchop consolidated, HLR D6 M1, Chest Supported Row repeated M2 D1+D4, Lateral Raise x3 per week M2, duplicate Band Pull-Apart warmups M3, duplicate Scap Push-Up M3)
Session order corrected: power always leads, conditioning always closes
D3 M1 restructured: arms removed, core + carries + shoulder health + Zone 2 only
D6 M1 upgraded: full arm block consolidated here from D3 + D2
D4 M1: Sissy Squat replaced with Leg Extension across all weeks
M2 D2: Med Ball Rotational Throws correctly placed before primary lift
M2 D4: Chest Supported Row replaced with Cable Row for variation
M2 D6: conditioning leads session, DB Lateral Raise removed
M2 (weeks 5-7): Flat DB Press added to D1 accessories — chest pattern maintained
M3 D5: Med Ball Slams + KB Swings correctly placed before Deficit Deadlift
M4 (weeks 13-15): full rewrite — single warmup, clean section order, shoulder health block, conditioning leads D6
GHR replaced with Nordic Ham Curl throughout all mesos
Deloads added: Week 8 (M2) and Week 12 (M3) — previously missing
Week 5 copy-paste error fixed — unique Dad Mission + Mindset per day
Shoulder health progression: M1 foundation → M2 adds serratus → M3 adds Trap 3 → M4 maintains peak stack

exercises.notes: Slow and controlled. Scapular activation.

3-4 RIR. Focus on stretch and squeeze.

3-4 RIR. Full ROM. Pause at stretch.

Explode laterally off one foot, land and absorb on the other. Soft knee at landing. Multi-directional reactive work introduced from week 1. Stay low, think speed not distance.

exercises.notes: Deload.

exercises.notes: Lie face-down on floor. Raise DBs out to sides, lead with elbows. Squeeze rear delts. Same stimulus as reverse pec deck.
Keep full ROM. Supinate at top.
DBs touching throughout. Elbows tight to body. Tricep-driven. Full lockout — squeeze hard at top.
No deload.

exercises.exercise: D1. 4 Rounds: 30s High Knees Sprint + 30s Rest
exercises.notes: 4 rounds. 30 seconds of maximum effort high knees or sprint in place. 30s rest. RPE 7-8.

exercises.reps: Today is for recharging. Spend quality time with your family without screens.

exercises.reps: Today's rest makes tomorrow's performance possible. Trust the process.
exercises.notes: Today's rest makes tomorrow's performance possible. Trust the process.
Easy pace. Outside if possible. This is not exercise — it’s active recovery. Promotes blood flow without adding fatigue. Go with family if you can.
Inhale 4s, hold 4s, exhale 4s, hold 4s. Repeat. Activates parasympathetic recovery. Do this before bed tonight if you skip it now.
Back knee on ground, front foot forward. Drive hip forward. Hip flexors take the most load from a training week. This is maintenance, not a bonus.
Forearm on doorframe, rotate away. Chest and anterior shoulder. Counteracts the internal rotation from bench, row, and desk posture.
Sit back on heels, reach one arm long, press armpit toward floor. Lats and thoracic spine. Complements the thoracic work on D3.

exercises.reps: Write a short note to your kid telling them something you're proud of about them.

data_6day.meso_tagline: Recover so you can build again

exercises.notes: Scapular activation.
Explosive push-up — hands leave floor, clap, land controlled. Same horizontal pressing power intent. Regress: Explosive Push-Up (no clap).
Elevate upper back on folded blanket (15-20cm) for slight incline angle. Or standard floor press — still a direct horizontal press.
Loop band around fixed anchor at shoulder height. Pull elbows past hips. Heavier band = more load. Or: Heavy Dead-Stop DB Row.
Lie face-down on floor or folded blanket under chest. Row both DBs. Eliminates momentum — same chest-supported intent. Retract scaps fully.
Lie on floor. Wide arc, slight elbow bend. Squeeze at top. Use slow tempo to compensate for limited ROM vs cable.
Same movement, same cues. Light DB, controlled raise. No shrugging.
Flat chest pattern maintained through M2. Full ROM.
Loop band around fixed anchor at face height (door handle). Pull to ears, pause 1s, external rotation arc. No anchor: DB Rear Delt Fly prone.
Elbow at 90°, upper arm pinned to side. Rotate forearm out. Slow and controlled. Same movement.
Elbows on wall, slide up. Upward rotation focus.

exercises.reps: Ask your child what made them laugh today — then really listen.
exercises.notes: Ask your child what made them laugh today — then really listen.

exercises.reps: Power is strength expressed with speed. Train with intent — every rep matters.
exercises.notes: Power is strength expressed with speed. Train with intent — every rep matters.
Activation.
Max height jump from standing. Land soft, reset fully. Same CNS primer intent as box jumps.
Hold light DB with both hands. Rotate explosively through hips — mimic a throw. Hips lead, arms follow. Both directions.

After box jumps — CNS primed. Sprint mechanics: shin angle, arm drive, hip extension. Not a max sprint — 80% effort. Teaching the pattern. This is sprint development starting 8 weeks before M4 demands it.
DBs racked on shoulders, elbows high. Goblet hold also works. Upright torso — same demand as front squat.
Hold one heavy DB in both hands, slightly off-centre. Challenges anti-lateral flexion. Alternate side each set.
RIR 3. Unilateral durability. Stretch at bottom.
RIR 3. Direct glute work. Full hip extension at top.
Hold one DB on working side. Stand on folded mat edge for ROM. Full stretch at bottom, full contraction at top.
Kneel on mat. Feet anchored under sofa or heavy stacked DBs. Lower body forward under control. Catch at bottom. Eccentric priority.
Ankle durability. 1 set — volume freed for lateral work.
M2: ↑ height from M1. Explode laterally, absorb landing with control. Reactive multi-directional power.

↑ control from M1. Drive rear knee lower. Full pelvic rotation.
↑ range from M1. Stack shoulder over shoulder at top.
↑ reps from M1. Directly improves front squat depth and sprint shin angle.
New in M2. Lunge + reach + rotate. Hip flexor + t-spine + ankle combined.
↑ from M1 (3 sets). Protract/retract fully.
Keep full ROM. Supinate at top.
One DB, both hands. Overhead. Full stretch, full lockout. Long head emphasis.
RIR 3. Brachialis focus.
One DB, both hands. Full overhead stretch. Long head. Full ROM.
Core stability. Brace before every rep.
Lie on back, arms to ceiling. Alternate lowering opposite arm and leg while bracing. Anti-extension + anti-rotation. Best no-anchor core sub.
One DB, one hand. Tall posture. Direct substitution.
Hold one DB with both hands. Rotate from high to low diagonally. Hips drive the rotation — not the arms.
HR 130-150 bpm. Nasal breathing. Bike, walk, or row.

Same movement, same cues. Light DB, controlled raise. No shrugging.

subtitle: The Standard. No rack required.
program_intro: This program is built for the garage. The spare room. The 5am session before the house wakes up. You have dumbbells and a few bands. That's enough. The same six-day structure, the same four mesos, the same progression logic — rebuilt for the floor. Some movements will feel different. The effort required is identical. Use the heaviest dumbbells you own on primary lifts. The load is always the variable.
equipment: Chair or sofa (for elevated foot exercises)
notes: The Home Standard is the dumbbell-only companion to The Standard. Every session, every meso, every progression — rebuilt for your garage, spare room, or living room floor. The structure is identical. The equipment is not. You don't need a rack to become The Standard.
